featureInfo.featureName: TaskFlow Pro - Core Platform (EPIC-1) and AI Prioritization Engine (EPIC-2)
featureInfo.environment: staging (AWS us-east-1, 3x t3.large instances, RDS PostgreSQL r5.large)

content.executiveSummary: NFR assessment reveals strong performance and security posture with minor concerns in reliability under extreme load. API response times meet SLA targets with 15% headroom. Security scan shows zero critical/high vulnerabilities. WebSocket sync P99 latency is 420ms against 500ms target. Primary concern is database connection pool exhaustion under 10x normal load (observed during stress test). Recommendation: proceed to production with connection pool monitoring and auto-scaling rules.

nfrRequirements.requirement: API response time P95 under 200ms for all CRUD operations
Zero critical or high vulnerabilities in SAST/DAST scans

performance.summary: All performance targets met. API CRUD operations P95 at 180ms (target <200ms). WebSocket sync P99 at 420ms (target <500ms). Page load time under 2s. AI prioritization batch processing completes within acceptable timeframes.

responseTime.notes: Measured across 50,000 requests over 1-hour load test. Create operations slightly slower at P95 195ms due to validation overhead. Read operations fastest at P50 22ms.

throughput.notes: 35% headroom above target. Throughput stable for 60 minutes with no degradation. Database becomes bottleneck at ~2,000 req/s.

resourceUsage.notes: All resource metrics well within targets. Memory usage stable over 24-hour soak test, no leaks detected.

scalability.horizontalScaling: Tested with 1, 3, and 6 instances. Linear throughput scaling up to 4 instances, sub-linear beyond due to database connection pooling. WebSocket sticky sessions work correctly with load balancer.
scalability.verticalScaling: Tested on t3.medium, t3.large, and t3.xlarge. Performance scales linearly with CPU cores for compute-bound operations.

loadTestResults.results: P95 520ms, 0.3% errors, 6,800 req/s - DB pool exhaustion observed

bottlenecks.bottleneck: Database connection pool exhaustion at 10,000 CCU
bottlenecks.impact: 0.3% request failures with 503 errors, P95 exceeds target
bottlenecks.recommendation: Increase connection pool from 50 to 100, implement connection pooling with PgBouncer, add auto-scaling rule for database read replicas

security.summary: Zero critical and high vulnerabilities. 2 medium and 4 low findings from SAST/DAST scans, all with remediation plans. Authentication uses industry-standard JWT with RS256. All data encrypted in transit (TLS 1.3) and at rest (AES-256).

authentication.mechanism: JWT with RS256 signing, refresh token rotation, 15-minute access token TTL
authentication.assessment: Authentication implementation follows OWASP best practices. Token refresh mechanism properly invalidates old refresh tokens. Brute-force protection via rate limiting (5 attempts per 15 minutes).
findings: Refresh token stored in httpOnly secure cookie - good practice
Token rotation working correctly on refresh

authorization.mechanism: RBAC with project-level scoping. Roles: owner, admin, member, viewer
authorization.assessment: Authorization checks at both API gateway and service levels. All endpoints require valid authentication. Resource-level authorization verified for task CRUD operations.
findings: RBAC enforcement at service layer prevents privilege escalation

dataProtection.encryptionAtRest: AES-256 via AWS RDS encryption. All S3 buckets use SSE-S3. Encryption verified via AWS Config rules.
dataProtection.encryptionInTransit: TLS 1.3 enforced at load balancer. Inter-service communication uses mTLS. No plaintext HTTP endpoints.
dataProtection.piiHandling: PII fields (email, name) encrypted at application level with field-level encryption. PII audit logging in place. GDPR data deletion endpoint implemented.
findings: Field-level encryption adds ~5ms latency to PII queries - acceptable

vulnerabilities.vulnerability: Express.js version 4.18.2 has a known ReDoS vulnerability in path-to-regexp dependency (CVE-2024-45296)
vulnerabilities.remediation: Update path-to-regexp to 0.1.10 or upgrade Express to 4.21.0+

vulnerabilities.vulnerability: Missing Content-Security-Policy header on WebSocket upgrade endpoint
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add CSP header to WebSocket upgrade handler

securityScans.summary: 1,247 files scanned, 0 critical, 1 medium, 3 low findings
Full scan of API surface, 0 critical, 1 medium, 1 low finding
142 dependencies scanned, 1 medium vulnerability in path-to-regexp

compliance.assessment: SOC 2 controls for data access, encryption, and audit logging are in place. GDPR data subject rights (access, deletion, portability) implemented. Privacy policy updated.

gaps.gap: Cookie consent banner not yet implemented for EU users
gaps.remediation: Implement cookie consent banner with granular opt-in before GA launch

reliability.summary: System meets 99.9% availability target during 30-day staging observation. Error rate under 0.01% at normal load. Circuit breakers and retry mechanisms functioning correctly. Disaster recovery plan tested and RPO/RTO targets met.

availability.uptimeData: Measured over 30-day staging period (Sep 5 - Oct 5). Two planned maintenance windows excluded. One unplanned 12-minute outage due to database failover.

faultTolerance.assessment: Circuit breakers configured for all external dependencies (database, Redis, AI service). Retry with exponential backoff for transient failures. Graceful degradation: AI features disabled when AI service unavailable, core task management continues to work.
mechanisms: Dead letter queue for failed events

chaosTestResults.test: Kill one of three application instances
chaosTestResults.findings: Health check interval could be reduced from 10s to 5s for faster detection

disasterRecovery.backupStrategy: Continuous WAL archiving to S3 with daily full snapshots. Point-in-time recovery tested to arbitrary timestamps. Cross-region backup replication to us-west-2.

maintainability.summary: Codebase is well-structured with consistent patterns. Test coverage at 89% statements. Technical debt is manageable with clear remediation plans. Documentation is comprehensive for API and architecture.

codeQuality.assessment: TypeScript strict mode enabled. ESLint with custom rule set. Cyclomatic complexity average 4.2 (target <10). Code duplication at 2.1% (target <3%). Zero eslint errors, 12 warnings (all non-critical).

technicalDebt.assessment: Technical debt ratio at 3.2% (target <5%). Main debt items: legacy task migration code, incomplete TypeScript typing in WebSocket handlers, TODO comments for optimization opportunities.

majorItems.item: Legacy task migration code that can be removed after migration completes

majorItems.impact: Better IDE support and fewer runtime type errors

accessibility.assessment: Automated accessibility audit via axe-core shows 0 critical, 0 serious, 3 moderate issues. Keyboard navigation fully functional. Screen reader tested with NVDA. Color contrast ratios meet AA standards.

findings.issue: Missing aria-label on task priority icon buttons
findings.remediation: Add descriptive aria-label to priority icon buttons

quickWins.improvement: Add aria-labels to 8 icon buttons across TaskCard and FilterBar components
quickWins.implementation: Add aria-label prop to IconButton components in TaskCard.tsx and FilterBar.tsx

quickWins.improvement: Update path-to-regexp dependency to close medium severity CVE
quickWins.implementation: npm update path-to-regexp or upgrade Express to 4.21.0

recommendedActions.action: Increase database connection pool size and implement PgBouncer for connection pooling to handle 10K concurrent users without pool exhaustion
recommendedActions.impact: Eliminates the only performance bottleneck observed at 10K CCU, prevents 503 errors under extreme load

recommendedActions.action: Implement cookie consent mechanism for GDPR compliance before EU launch
recommendedActions.impact: Required for GDPR compliance in EU markets

monitoringHooks.description: Track P95 response time across all API endpoints to detect performance degradation early
monitoringHooks.alertAction: Page on-call engineer via PagerDuty if P95 exceeds 200ms for 5 consecutive minutes
monitoringHooks.implementation: Prometheus histogram metric with Grafana dashboard and PagerDuty alert rule

monitoringHooks.description: Monitor connection pool usage to prevent exhaustion under load spikes
monitoringHooks.alertAction: Auto-scale read replicas when pool utilization exceeds 70%. Page DBA when exceeding 90%.
monitoringHooks.implementation: PgBouncer stats exposed via Prometheus exporter

findings.finding: DB connection pool exhaustion at 10K CCU
2 medium vulnerabilities in dependencies and headers
findings.recommendation: Update dependencies and add CSP header

conditions: Increase DB connection pool before GA
Implement cookie consent for EU launch
signOff.comments: Strong NFR posture overall. The database connection pool concern is the only item blocking unrestricted 10K user support. Approve for limited beta with 5K user cap while PgBouncer is implemented.
